comments.undefined: Ontology for the Biological and Chemical Oceanography Data Management Office (BCO-DMO).

comment.undefined: A list of datasets in a catalog that isn't BCO-DMO
A term in some ControlledVocabulary with a community-agreed definition
Programming languages such as Python, Go, Rust and Java.
A file format  used to bundle multiple files and folders into a single file for easier storage or transfer.
A formal metadata profile specification to which the datapackage conforms.
A context-specific description of a file. The context could be data, metadata or supplemental documentation.

One or more statuses of a BCO-DMO Entity. This is primarily used to determine where an entity appears - not at all while it's being contructed, in draft review for a submitter, published, deprecated or unpublished.
A metadata file (e.g. ISO 19115-2 XML, dataset description PDF) describing a dataset.
A frame of reference for when something existed or occupied time — it is either a durationless point (an instant) or a thing with some extent in time (an interval).
A functional role performed by an agent (e.g. Principal Investigator, Co-Investigator).
A piece of software that is deployed and executable.
An instrument as referenced within a specific dataset, with dataset-specific properties.
The group of people attached to a BCO-DMO Submission.
Intended audience for an item, i.e. the group for whom the item was created or is being offered to.
An internal BCO-DMO identifier (version 2 scheme).
The connection between a dataset and the award the funded its generation

Dataset Parameter Attribute: Primary Longtiude Variable

comment.undefined: An attribute of a dataset parameter that provides more context as to how it could be used.
A platform (vessel, buoy, glider, etc.) used for data collection.
A BCO-DMO specific concept
A versioned BCO-DMO dataset that has been assigned a DOI.
A specific version of a Dataset that has been stored  in an archive that typcially provides access
Identifiers from the Coldfusion version of OSPREY
Identifiers from the Drupal version of OSPREY
A named scheme or authority that mints identifiers (DOI, ROR, ORCID, etc.).
A link between two entities that are related in some way as defined by rdf:predicate. This entity allows for annotating details of the match.
A string value

comment.undefined: Representative of a type of image file.
An image that is representative of some thing

A physical file (byte stream) associated with a dataset or package.

Results from synthesis of other works
The dataset is some kind of document.
Results from other field work
The dataset is a cruise report
The dataset is the result of executing a model
The dataset is experimental results.
A category describing the type of dataset (e.g. Cruise Data, Model Output).
A time period with a beginning and an end, used to describe dataset temporal coverage.
A curated collection, set, or list of some Things.
A service that offers access to some CreativeWork by implementing a MediaService
A research vessel cruise, a specialisation of Platform for vessel-type platforms.
An item in a collection
A comment or some other piece of content that annotates for some specific purpose some Thing
A human individual associated with some entity.
The DOI is registered and publicly resolvable (Registered with DataCite).
The DOI record has been created locally but not yet registered.

Initiating the DOI metadata publication failed.
DOI metadata submission to the minter is in progress.
DOI metadata publication failed after initiation.
The lifecycle status of a DOI Dataset (Draft, Reserved, Published, etc.).
An axis-aligned bounding box geometry defined by west/south/east/north boundaries. A set of Point geometries where the first Point and the last point are the same Point.
Something that describes a spatial element
A dataset which may be internal or external to BCO-DMO.
A scholarly publication related to a dataset (journal article, report, etc.).
A string that is used as an identifier
A unit of measure associated with a dataset parameter.
A grouping category for resource relation types.
A category of platform (e.g. vessel, mooring, glider).
A parameter as instantiated within a specific dataset column, with dataset-level name, description, units, and format.
An organisational body such as a publisher, funder, or identifier authority.
An image file.
A  license applied to some creative work.
A scientific deployment (cruise, expedition, mooring deployment) during which data were collected.
A URL
An abstract spatial geometry.
an ordered collection of observations indexed by time, representing the temporal evolution of one or more properties of a feature, system, or phenomenon.
An access or governance policy applied to a resource.
A named title/designation for a platform (e.g. the full name of a research vessel).
A cryptographic hash (e.g. MD5) used as a file integrity checksum.
A spatial reference system
An email address.
A Research Organization Registry identifier.
A funding award (grant) from a funder that supports data collection.
A qualified association that links an Agent to a role and optionally an affiliation context.
A file format type, characterised by a label and MIME type (e.g. CSV, XML, PDF).

A static or dynamic scenario. A state of affairs that describes some configuration of the world. Most often a situation describes the interaction of 2 or more entities. In some cases these interactions are time bounded and can be labeled as significant events.

A note or piece of text typically used for Annotations

An activity that enables research. The research typically produces datasets.

A web page

A supplemental (non-primary) document file associated with a dataset.

An affordance made to some audience by a provider to access all or part of some CreativeWork. Often times it is an interpretation of the whole work - a metadata record, a visualization, a file in a specific format, etc.

A funded scientific project under which data were collected.
A Dataset connected to another CreativeWork through some ResourceRelation
A boolean value like True or False, Yes or No, 1 or 0.

A list of Datasets

The motivation for annotating some thing.
The mailing address.
A related CreativeWork to some other CreativeWork. This describes the situation surrounding this relationship.

An entity that should no longer be used.
Any work that can be created by some Agent
The Dataset's data is not available

The status of a creative work in terms of its stage in a lifecycle. Example terms include Incomplete, Draft, Published, Obsolete. Some organizations define a set of terms for the stages of their publication lifecycle.
